completed_steps.summary: Classified the repository as a single-part web monolith built with Next.js App Router and TypeScript.
Found the generic root README plus an adjacent BMAD project context artifact; no existing docs/index.md was present.
Identified the primary stack as Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS 4, motion/react, ESLint, Vitest, and Testing Library.
Scanned runtime source files and confirmed that the app has a centralized content module, one client presentation component, SEO metadata routes, and no internal API or data model layer.
Generated the annotated source tree and documented critical directories, entrypoints, and non-runtime BMAD tooling folders.
Reviewed deployment and operations signals; found no committed CI/CD or hosting configuration beyond generic README guidance and a .vercel ignore entry.
Confirmed that this is a single-part app with no cross-part integration architecture to document.
Wrote the architecture document covering runtime flow, module boundaries, styling, SEO, testing, and safe extension points.
Generated the project overview, component inventory, and development guide based on the exhaustive code scan.
Created the master index with navigation to all generated docs and adjacent artifacts.
Validated the generated documentation set and found no incomplete-document markers requiring follow-up generation.

technology_stack.tech_summary: Next.js 16.1.6 + React 19.2.3 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS 4 + motion/react + Vitest

batches_completed.summary: Captured the route shell, metadata routes, favicon, global styles, and the homepage test.
Captured the single client-rendered portfolio component and its animation patterns.
Captured the central content source of truth and URL helper.
Captured the content contracts used by the app.
Captured shared test setup including the IntersectionObserver mock.
Cataloged static SVG assets, including the social preview image.

resume_instructions: Workflow is complete. Start a new initial_scan or full_rescan when the codebase changes materially.
